items.name: Classic Pomodoro (25/5)
items.description: 25 minutes of work, 5-minute break, longer break after 4 sessions
items.best_for: Getting started with focus techniques, tasks you tend to procrastinate on
items.pros: Low barrier to entry, creates urgency, easy to track
items.cons: Can interrupt flow states, too short for complex debugging
items.best_for_devs: Code reviews, documentation, small bug fixes

items.name: Extended Pomodoro (50/10)
items.best_for: Medium-complexity tasks that need sustained focus
items.pros: Longer flow states, fewer interruptions from breaks
items.cons: Harder to maintain discipline, requires stronger focus habits

items.name: Flowmodoro (Flow Time)
items.description: Work until you naturally lose focus, then take a break proportional to work duration
items.best_for: Experienced developers who can self-regulate focus
items.pros: Respects natural flow states, no forced interruptions
items.cons: Easy to skip breaks, harder to track, requires self-awareness
items.best_for_devs: Architecture work, deep debugging, creative problem-solving

items.name: Deep Work Blocks (90 min)
items.description: 90-minute focused sessions with 20-minute breaks (based on ultradian rhythms)
items.best_for: Complex, high-value work that benefits from extended concentration
items.pros: Maximum flow depth, produces highest-quality output
items.cons: Requires strong focus habits, very demanding, can't sustain all day
items.best_for_devs: System design, performance optimization, writing RFCs

items.name: Timeboxing
items.description: Set a fixed time limit for a task and stop when time expires, regardless of completion
items.best_for: Preventing perfectionism, ensuring balanced time allocation
items.pros: Prevents scope creep, creates accountability, forces prioritization
items.cons: May produce incomplete work, requires follow-up sessions
items.best_for_devs: Prototyping, exploring new technologies, sprint tasks

comparisons.insight: The best focus technique is the one you'll actually use consistently. Start with classic Pomodoro, then experiment with longer durations as your focus stamina improves.

keyInsight: Pomodoro is a starting point, not a destination. As your focus stamina improves, graduate to longer sessions. The real value is having ANY structured break system — the specific duration matters less than consistency.
